title: dryopteris nymphalis
intro: Dryopteris nymphalis, also known as the Wood Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of certain flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Dryopteris nymphalis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its possible use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used in salves and creams for topical application.
